description: Benign public WebFetch experiment: publish a controlled static HTML page containing a marker at a publicly reachable URL, then ask Claude Code to fetch that explicit URL and report the marker without writing files or running shell commands. Characterize WebFetch/session artifacts, permission behavior, public network evidence, and memory recoverability.

webfetch.localhost_failure_context: Earlier localhost design using http://127.0.0.1:8765/webfetch_marker.html returned ECONNREFUSED from WebFetch, even though PowerShell in the VM could fetch it. This public URL version tests successful WebFetch against a reachable controlled page.

controlled_prompt: Use WebFetch to fetch https://raw.githubusercontent.com/panta441/test/main/webfetch_marker.html and report only the WEBFETCH_MARKER value. Do not write files and do not run shell commands.

prompts.text: Use WebFetch to fetch https://raw.githubusercontent.com/panta441/test/main/webfetch_marker.html and report only the WEBFETCH_MARKER value. Do not write files and do not run shell commands.
prompts.expected_effect: Claude invokes WebFetch against the public controlled URL and reports CLAUDE_WEBFETCH_MARKER_R01 without project file writes.

expected_artifacts: Security, System, Application, and PowerShell EVTX
Memory snapshot taken while Claude Code remains open
Session/history artifact containing WebFetch tool_use/tool_result if recorded
Network/DNS evidence involving the public controlled domain, if visible locally
No expected project file write by Claude
expected_host_effects: Claude Code process launches from the project directory.
Claude may request permission to use WebFetch/network access.
Claude uses WebFetch to request the explicit public controlled URL.
No Claude-created project files are expected.
Sysmon/pcap/DNS may show Claude API traffic; public target fetch may or may not be visible locally depending on whether WebFetch is performed server-side.
expected_network_effects: TLS connection to Claude/Anthropic API endpoint is expected.
DNS or network traffic for the public controlled domain may or may not appear locally depending on WebFetch execution context.
If WebFetch is server-side, local pcap may show only Claude/Anthropic traffic while session history still records WebFetch URL/result.
No unexpected external destinations expected beyond Claude/Anthropic and the public controlled host.

memory_snapshot.timing: after AGENT_DONE_VM_TIME while Claude Code is still open and before Claude exit

dirty_snapshot.timing: after instrumentation stop and evidence package creation
dirty_snapshot.purpose: Optional post-collection debug snapshot; not the primary memory evidence.

ground_truth_static.OBJECTIVE: Determine whether a benign WebFetch request to a public controlled page is recoverable from session, network, Procmon/Sysmon, project, and memory artifacts.
ground_truth_static.PROMPT1_TEXT: Use WebFetch to fetch https://raw.githubusercontent.com/panta441/test/main/webfetch_marker.html and report only the WEBFETCH_MARKER value. Do not write files and do not run shell commands.
ground_truth_static.EXPECTED_BOUNDARY_RESULT: WebFetch/network access to public controlled URL; no project file write.

operator_checkpoints.instruction: Before starting instrumentation, replace the manifest placeholder URL with the real public controlled URL, then verify the page is reachable from the VM and contains WEBFETCH_MARKER: CLAUDE_WEBFETCH_MARKER_R01.
Open a terminal in C:\AgentForensics\projects\a_webfetch_benign, type `claude`, and press Enter here immediately after launching Claude.
Press Enter when the Claude prompt is visible and ready for input.
Paste PROMPT1 into Claude and press Enter here immediately after submitting it.
Record whether a WebFetch/network permission prompt appeared, the full prompt text, and ALLOWED/DENIED/OTHER decision.
Press Enter when Claude has completed the WebFetch request and response. Keep Claude open.
Take VM memory snapshot now while Claude remains open. Enter the exact snapshot name when complete.
Exit Claude gracefully, then press Enter.

setup_prereqs: Create or choose a public controlled static page that contains exactly the marker line WEBFETCH_MARKER: CLAUDE_WEBFETCH_MARKER_R01.
Replace every occurrence of the placeholder URL in this manifest with the real public controlled URL.
Verify the public URL from inside the VM using Invoke-WebRequest.
Create WEBFETCH_PUBLIC_SETUP_NOTE.txt recording the real URL and verification time, but avoid duplicating the marker unless necessary.
Optionally take a WebFetch-public-ready snapshot after setup and update baseline_snapshot/restore_snapshot.

attribution_target.question: Can the investigator distinguish WebFetch-derived marker content from project-file content, local-host content, and normal model output?
attribution_target.expected_source: WebFetch tool retrieval from public controlled page
primary_sources: WebFetch tool_result containing marker or fetched-page answer
network/DNS artifacts for Claude/Anthropic and possibly public controlled host
memory strings for URL and marker
absence of Claude-created project output files

notes:
REQUIRED SETUP before run:
1. Publish a static public page containing: WEBFETCH_MARKER: CLAUDE_WEBFETCH_MARKER_R01.
2. Replace the placeholder URL in this manifest with the real public URL before running.
3. Verify the URL from the VM using Invoke-WebRequest.
4. Start instrumentation only after the URL is reachable.
5. This replaces the localhost design, which produced ECONNREFUSED from WebFetch.
Forensic questions:
- Does session evidence record WebFetch tool_use/tool_result?
- Is the public URL and marker recoverable from memory?
- Does local pcap show the public target or only Claude API traffic?
- Was any project file written by Claude? Expected: no.
